# stat-api Java SDK

Official Java client for the [stat-api](https://stat-api.com) sports data API —
a single, typed surface over NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, and PGA data.

Zero runtime dependencies (JDK 17 `java.net.http` + a hand-written JSON reader).
Version 0.1.0.

## Install

Maven Central is deferred, and JitPack can't yet resolve this SDK: it ships in the
`java/` subdirectory of a monorepo, and JitPack needs a root build config for a
subdirectory module (also deferred). Install from source for now — clone the repo
and build the [`java/` subdirectory](https://github.com/stat-api/stat-api/tree/main/java).

When JitPack is wired up, the coordinate will be
`com.github.stat-api:stat-api`:

```kotlin
repositories {
    maven("https://jitpack.io")
}
dependencies {
    // Deferred — needs a root build config for the java/ subdirectory module.
    implementation("com.github.stat-api:stat-api:v0.1.0")
}
```

## Authenticate

The client reads your API key from the `STAT_API_KEY` environment
variable, or you can pass it directly:

```java
import com.statapi.StatApi;

StatApi api = new StatApi();                 // reads STAT_API_KEY
StatApi keyed = new StatApi("sdb_...");      // or pass it in
```

Point at a different host with the two-arg constructor or the builder
(`STAT_API_BASE_URL`, default `https://api.stat-api.com`):

```java
StatApi custom = StatApi.builder()
    .apiKey("sdb_...")
    .baseUrl("https://api.stat-api.com")
    .timeout(java.time.Duration.ofSeconds(60))
    .build();
```

## List, get, and iterate

```java
import com.statapi.Page;
import com.statapi.nba.NBAPlayer;
import com.statapi.nba.NBAPlayersListParams;

// One page.
Page<NBAPlayer> page = api.nba().players().list(new NBAPlayersListParams().limit(25L));
for (NBAPlayer player : page.rows()) {
    System.out.println(player.fullName());
}

// Fetch one row by id.
NBAPlayer player = api.nba().players().get(201939L);

// Auto-paginate the whole result set (walks next_from_id for you).
for (NBAPlayer p : api.nba().players().iterate(new NBAPlayersListParams().teamId(1))) {
    System.out.println(p.fullName());
}
```

Rows are `record`s — access fields via camelCase accessors (`player.teamId()`).
High-precision columns (dates, timestamps, decimals) arrive as `String` so no
precision is lost on the wire.

## Errors

Every non-2xx response throws a typed `StatApiException` subclass; the parsed
server envelope is on `.body()`.

```java
import com.statapi.NotFoundException;
import com.statapi.QuotaExceededException;
import com.statapi.StatApiException;

try {
    api.nba().players().get(-1L);
} catch (NotFoundException e) {
    // ...
} catch (QuotaExceededException e) {
    System.out.println("quota resets at " + e.resetsAt() + " -> " + e.upgradeUrl());
} catch (StatApiException e) {
    System.out.println(e.status() + " " + e.body());
}
```

| Status | Exception |
|-------|-----------|
| 400 | `ValidationException` |
| 401 | `AuthenticationException` |
| 402 | `PlanRequiredException` |
| 404 | `NotFoundException` |
| 429 | `QuotaExceededException` |
| other | `StatApiException` |

## Quota

Every response carries your monthly record quota. It's on each `Page` and on
`QuotaExceededException`:

```java
Page<NBAPlayer> page = api.nba().players().list(new NBAPlayersListParams().limit(1L));
if (page.quota() != null) {
    System.out.println(page.quota().remaining() + " of " + page.quota().limit() + " left");
}
```
